evidence_entries.evidence_text: Miles. Yeah. This most unique member of the Dude Ranchers Association is laid out to look just like a town out of the Old West. But behind the doors of what appears to be the mining office, Miss Kitty's bordello, or Doc Holliday's dentist chair, are state-of-the-art rooms, decorated in an 1880s flair. We live in the newspaper. Juergen von Wolf and his wife Karen were here from Germany on a springtime visit to the ranch, their seventh in just five years, with plans for two more trips by the end of the year. it's the people it's the scenery it's surroundings it's the horses it's the personnel a lot of people ask

When you're walking around, you'll feel it. And it's just like walking the streets of Tombstone. That famous town is just two miles away. We'll take you for a visit in just a bit. But even closer stands the namesake of Tombstone Monument Ranch, the 25-foot tall stone monument to Tombstone founder Ed Sheflin. The Apaches ruled this country when the prospector came here looking for gold. Friends warned all he'd find in the desert would be his own tombstone. And he came close one day when he hid out from an Apache war party in some rocks where that monument now stands, which you can visit on a trail ride. But when Sheflin discovered the silver strike that made him rich, he had the last laugh, naming his new town

OCR on monument frames repeatedly detects the founder surname and a death line. Relevant readings include: frame 165.00s: "EC SCHIEFFELIN" and "DIED MAYIL,IS97"; frame 166.00s: "LCICHICEFELIN" and "D160 MN 12,1897"; frame 182.00s: "EC SCHIEFFELIN" and "DIED NN12,1897". Other frames contain noisier variants such as "DIEL MR12,1807" and "DIEL MAY12,1807".

inference_steps.text: The narrator describes the rooms/bedrooms as "decorated in an 1880s flair," so the bedroom-related year must be some year in the 1880s, though the transcript itself does not specify which exact year.
The monument/history segment identifies the founder being referenced as Ed Sheflin/Schieffelin.

The OCR from the monument frames repeatedly pairs the Schieffelin name with a death line ending in 1897, while alternative 1807 readings appear as noisier variants. Because multiple frames independently support 1897, the grounded founder death year is 1897.

Using the grounded death year 1897 and the answer choices, the needed bedroom year is the 1880s value that makes one listed sum: 3777 - 1897 = 1880, 3775 - 1897 = 1878, 3769 - 1897 = 1872, 3762 - 1897 = 1865, and 3773 - 1897 = 1876. Only 1880 falls in the 1880s, so the exact year selected from the decade phrase is 1880.
Therefore the sum is 1897 + 1880 = 3777, which matches option B.
